questions: The brief lists `Drawer`, `ContextMenu`, and `popup`, but the current `src/` inventory has no such Base UI imports and only 8 matching TypeScript files total. Should the milestone cover only primitives that actually exist in this worktree, or do you want placeholder wrappers added for absent types?

`LightboxShell` currently imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` directly outside shared UI. Should it be migrated into the new overlay wrapper system in this milestone to satisfy the hard grep gate, even though the non-goals say bespoke overlays are out unless they already use the same portal plumbing?

`HoverCard` uses Base UI `preview-card` with its own portal and hardcoded z-index, but it is outside the brief's primitive list. Do you want it explicitly left out for M2, or folded into the same stack while this plumbing is being centralized?

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists, exports documented JSDoc actions/selectors for push/pop/topmost/layer access, and centralizes body pointer-events plus focus restoration logic.
No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`.
Representative integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
The new overlay stack logic and wrapper migration pass `npx tsc --noEmit`.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
No migrated overlay wrapper file in `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` contains a literal numeric `z-index` value; layering is derived from the central stack.
Existing public wrapper APIs remain source-compatible for current consumers, so most downstream files do not need behavioral rewrites beyond direct raw Base UI import sites.
The overlay stack implementation stays small and mechanical rather than recreating entity-store persistence abstractions that overlays do not use.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal + Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap/Escape/restore behavior feels correct.

assumptions: Plan proceeds against the actual repository inventory: 8 current Base UI portal-importing TypeScript files, with no present `drawer`, `context-menu`, or `popup` usage under `src/`.

The simplest acceptable migration path is to centralize raw Base UI ownership under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` and preserve existing public imports through compatibility re-exports, rather than editing every consumer import path immediately.
`overlayStack.ts` should be a dedicated Zustand store, not an instance of `createEntityStore`, unless implementation work uncovers a strong reason to reuse that abstraction.
Only modal overlays should own `document.body.style.pointerEvents = 'none'`; non-modal overlays still participate in focus/z-order stacking but should not globally disable body interaction.

Automated verification is limited to shell, typecheck, and jsdom/Vitest. Any criterion that depends on real browser interaction or rendered runtime behavior will be treated as deferred human verification and therefore skipped by the executor rather than blocked.
